page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-16 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 60 GB of RAM, translating to 3.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz, the instance allocates 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the c4-standard-16 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance. It achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression (35.06 GB/sec), single-threaded bzip3 decompression (14.89 GB/sec), and Geekbench single-core testing (2269.0). Text processing and small-model LLM text generation (330.39 tokens/sec) also rank in the top tier. Multi-core operations, such as Geekbench multi-core (13313.0) and stress-ng multi-core (14602.98), fall into the average tier, alongside memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations (24.75 million operations/sec).

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and large L3 cache provide stable, predictable compute performance without the overhead of shared-core architectures. This makes the instance highly efficient for compute-bound workloads that benefit from advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The c4-standard-16 is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, text processing, and light language model inference tasks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 60 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Featuring 8 physical cores with hyperthreading and a substantial 260 MB L3 cache, this instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing, single-threaded compression, and small-scale LLM text generation. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with mid-range benchmarks. This resource profile makes the c4-standard-16 highly effective for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate data processing tasks where dedicated CPU execution is required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-16 is an x86_64 server with 16 vCPUs, 60 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering top-tier single-core compression and text processing performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-16 features 16 vCPUs, 60 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for compute-intensive and single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon instance delivering top-tier single-core execution and advanced matrix extensions for compute-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 60 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier single-core performance in text processing and gzip compression
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and light LLM inference
